stemHtml:
A low time pilot finds him/herself under a low cloud base hand flying a piston engine two-seater aircraft on a flight plan to the destination airport in worsening weather conditions. He/she becomes even more nervous after his passenger starts complaining because they should have landed more than hour ago to meet with friends at the airport.
Flying a very low-level circuit, the pilot lets the airspeed deteriorate to such an extent that the turn from base leg to final approach results in a stall and spin accident. Which of the following human factors aspect likely played the key role in the accident?
